narrative_votes: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
URW: Negative Consequences for the West
URW: Negative Consequences for the West
URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly and harshly criticizes and dehumanizes the people of a Western nation (America), aligning with the narrative of discrediting the West through extreme negative portrayal.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement attacks the legitimacy and character of Western governance, portraying unelected officials as malevolent, which fits the definition of discrediting Western political structures.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote explicitly accuses Western media of presenting a false narrative ('framing') about the war's progress, thereby undermining its reliability and suggesting it causes harm, which matches the 'Distrust towards Media' narrative.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This phrase labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' directly questioning its integrity and positioning an alternative source as truthful, which is a core element of fostering distrust in media.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, while presented in a fatalistic tone, evokes and amplifies fear about the potential for nuclear war and total annihilation, which aligns with the narrative of amplifying war-related fears.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind of Russia China`Britain (lots of europe) amerika canada australia NZ..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote predicts severe negative consequences ('horrors left behind') specifically for a list of Western and allied nations, highlighting adverse outcomes for the West as a result of the conflict.
The title and premise of the discussion explicitly predict a specific outcome of the war—that Russia is winning—which fits the narrative of speculating on war outcomes.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This direct quote is a severe, generalized criticism of Western (specifically American) society and character, portraying them as morally repugnant and inferior, which aligns with the narrative of discrediting the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion..
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement frames Western (American) foreign policy as fundamentally misguided and hysterical, suggesting it is based on false threats and leads to unnecessary conflict, which is a prediction/assertion of negative outcomes stemming from Western actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly accuses Western media of presenting a false narrative about the war's progress, claiming this misinformation has harmful real-world consequences, which undermines trust in media sources.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement, while presented with sarcastic despair, explicitly invokes the specter of nuclear war and total annihilation, amplifying fears about the ultimate, catastrophic consequence of the conflict.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: New Sarah Westall & Scott Ritter: Russia is Winning, Nuclear Threat is a Sign of Desperation & Very Serious
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The title of the content explicitly predicts a war outcome ('Russia is Winning') and speculates on the meaning and seriousness of the nuclear threat, which fits the definition of speculating about conflict results.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This direct quote is a severe and derogatory criticism of Western (specifically American) society and character, aligning with the narrative of discrediting the West by portraying its people as morally repugnant.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: The quote explicitly predicts and almost welcomes a catastrophic nuclear war outcome that would annihilate Western populations, highlighting a severe negative consequence for the West.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This statement directly accuses Western media of misleading framing that has harmful real-world consequences, undermining trust in its reporting on the Ukraine war.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This question, posed in the promotional text, evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear escalation, which is a core element of amplifying war-related fears.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Blaming the war on others rather than the invader
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This shifts blame for the continuation and death toll of the war away from Russia (the invader) and onto Western media and unnamed actors ('they') who are allegedly desperate to keep it going.
